data.title: Imagion Biosystems advances MagSense breast cancer trial toward U.S. Phase 2 start
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Imagion Biosystems Ltd. ( (AU:IBX) ) has shared an update.

Imagion Biosystems has submitted its first Investigational New Drug application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for a Phase 2 clinical trial of its MagSense imaging agent in HER2-positive breast cancer patients. The company has completed engagement with four U.S. clinical sites, progressed contract negotiations, and advanced manufacturing preparations so the study can start quickly once the IND is approved, which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

The planned Phase 2 trial will evaluate safety, dosing, imaging schedule and diagnostic performance across a three-part design, and is expected to conclude 18 to 24 months after IND acceptance. Results are intended not only to validate MagSense in detecting nodal metastases and inform its clinical and economic value, but also to generate quantitative imaging data to support AI diagnostic tools, while an amended convertible note with Mercer Street provides an additional $300,000 and extended maturities to help fund these activities.

More about Imagion Biosystems Ltd.

Imagion Biosystems Ltd. is an Australian medical technology company focused on improving cancer care through early detection using its proprietary MagSense imaging technology. The company is developing targeted imaging agents, with a primary focus on HER2-positive breast cancer, and is advancing its products through clinical trials to support regulatory approval and potential commercialization in oncology diagnostics.

Average Trading Volume: 1,744,631

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$10.3M

data.description:

The latest update is out from Marlin Global Ltd. ( (NZ:MLN) ).

Marlin Global Ltd., an NZX-listed investment company investing in offshore growth stocks, reported an unaudited net asset value of $0.8513 per share as at 21 April 2026, up from $0.8469 a week earlier. The share price closed at $0.8650, representing a 2% premium to NAV, with no impact from treasury stock as there are currently no shares held under its buyback programme.

The portfolio remains heavily weighted toward major U.S. technology and payments names, with Amazon and Microsoft each at 8%, Meta Platforms at 6%, and Mastercard and Nvidia at 5% apiece. The latest disclosure underscores Marlin Global’s continued reliance on large-cap global growth leaders to drive returns for investors, highlighting its concentrated exposure to high-profile tech and digital economy franchises.

More about Marlin Global Ltd.

Marlin Global Ltd. is a New Zealand-listed investment company focused on growth companies based outside New Zealand and Australia. Managed by specialist firm Fisher Funds, it aims to deliver competitive returns via capital growth and dividends, giving investors tax-efficient access to a diversified international portfolio listed on global exchanges or in select unlisted offshore companies.

data.title: Barys Resources Secures First Kazakh Gold Exploration Licence in North Balkhash Region
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kopore Metals Ltd ( (AU:BRY) ) has provided an update.

Barys Resources has secured its first exploration licence in Kazakhstan, covering 165 square kilometres in the North Balkhash region, a volcanic and intrusive rock terrain considered highly prospective for epithermal gold mineralisation. The licence area lies close to existing gold mines in a jurisdiction with a mining code modelled on Western Australia and a government supportive of foreign investment.

The company’s move into Kazakhstan follows a six-month, countrywide reconnaissance program that drew on extensive Soviet-era geological data and modern modelling and geophysical tools to select targets. Management says its experienced technical team and the region’s favourable geology, infrastructure and flat topography position Barys to pursue cost-effective exploration and potential future gold discoveries in a globally significant yet underexplored gold province.

More about Kopore Metals Ltd

Barys Resources Ltd is an ASX-listed exploration company focused on gold, operating through its 100% owned Kazakhstan subsidiary Steppe Rock Resources Ltd. The company targets epithermal gold mineralisation in geologically prospective regions, leveraging extensive geological databases and modern exploration techniques to identify new gold deposits in underexplored terrains.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$5.28M

Ju Teng International Holdings Limited ( (HK:3336) ) has shared an update.

Ju Teng International Holdings Limited requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange from 9:00 a.m. on 23 April 2026. The company is led by chairman and CEO Cheng Li-Yu and governed by a board that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

The trading halt was imposed pending the release of an announcement relating to Hong Kong’s Code on Takeovers and Mergers and Share Buy-backs, which will contain inside information. The move signals that a potential corporate action involving a takeover, merger or share buy-back is under consideration, and investors are awaiting further clarification on the transaction’s scope and impact.

data.title: Dicker Data Reshapes Executive Roles and Incentives to Sharpen Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Dicker Data Limited ( (AU:DDR) ).

Dicker Data has formalised Fiona Brown’s position as Managing Director, recognising the expanded leadership role she has held since the 2025 departure of founder David Dicker, while she continues as Executive Chair. Her remit covers long term strategy, risk and financial management, culture and major growth initiatives, and her remuneration has been updated to include a long term incentive aligned with other executives, but without a short term incentive component.

From FY26 the company will introduce deferral of 30% of short term incentives for its existing executive directors into share rights that vest over one and two years, subject to malus provisions to strengthen accountability and governance. The board intends to seek shareholder approval for issuing performance and share rights under its incentive schemes, signalling a shift toward greater equity based remuneration that ties senior management rewards more closely to Dicker Data’s long term performance and shareholder interests.

If shareholder approval is not obtained, the board may settle long term and deferred short term incentives in cash, on market share purchases or equivalent superannuation contributions, maintaining flexibility over capital management. Detailed adjustments to executive pay structures, including base salary, variable pay and maximum long term incentive opportunities, underscore the board’s focus on aligning executive remuneration with the company’s growth prospects and evolving governance expectations.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:DDR) stock is a Hold with a A$10.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dicker Data Limited stock, see the AU:DDR Stock Forecast page.

More about Dicker Data Limited

Dicker Data Limited is an Australian technology distributor listed on the ASX that focuses on supplying IT hardware, software and cloud solutions to resellers and partners. The company targets the enterprise, government and small to medium business markets, positioning itself as a key intermediary between global technology vendors and the local channel ecosystem.

Operating in a competitive IT distribution sector, Dicker Data emphasises sustainable growth, capital allocation discipline and strong governance as it scales. Its leadership team plays a central role in executing long term strategy, including acquisitions and business transformations aimed at consolidating its market position and expanding service offerings.

Average Trading Volume: 483,483

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: A$1.63B

data.title: Elegance Optical Faces Ongoing Trading Suspension After HKEX Review
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0907) ) has provided an update.

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited has been informed that the Listing Review Committee of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange has upheld an earlier decision to suspend trading in its shares, citing the company’s failure to maintain sufficient operations and assets to justify continued listing. The committee found that Elegance Optical’s recently revived eyewear business lacks a proven, coherent plan and sustainable track record, and that its new liquor trading venture is thin on signed contracts, overly reliant on a part-time consultant and unsupported by robust financial data, leaving the company’s asset base and financial position too weak to meet listing requirements.

In its review, the committee highlighted inconsistencies in Elegance Optical’s business model disclosures, questioned the viability of its profit forecasts, and noted that a key shareholder loan was not on normal commercial terms, underscoring concerns over the company’s financial health. As a result, the regulator concluded that, as of the February hearing, Elegance Optical had not demonstrated sufficient operational substance or asset value, prompting the continuation of the trading suspension and signalling heightened risk and uncertainty for shareholders and potential investors.

More about Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, historically operates in the eyewear sector and has recently re-focused on its eyewear business while also venturing into a liquor trading business. The company has sought to adopt an asset-light and branded model in eyewear and to build a new revenue stream through liquor trading, but both lines remain at an early stage with limited track record and unclear long-term sustainability.

YTD Price Performance: 12.96%

Average Trading Volume: 2,035,469

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$54.12M
